verses.text: If there arise a prophet or dreamer, and giveth a sign or wonder, and it come to pass — saying, Let us go after other gods — thou shalt not hearken. The LORD your God proveth you, to know whether ye love the LORD with all your heart. Ye shall walk after the LORD, cleave unto Him. That prophet shall be put to death; he hath spoken to turn you away. Guidance: A working miracle is not a credential — the message decides the messenger. Even a sign that comes true can be a TEST sent by God to expose the heart. Truth is not measured in wonders but in whether it points to the right God.

If thy brother, thy son, thy daughter, the wife of thy bosom, thy friend as thine own soul, entice thee secretly: Let us serve other gods — thou shalt not consent, neither pity, neither conceal him. He hath sought to thrust thee away from the LORD. All Israel shall hear, and fear, and do no more any such wickedness. Guidance: The hardest verses in the chapter. The covenant put loyalty to God above every blood tie. Modern readers feel the cut — and they should. The text names the closest possible relations to make the point: no human love rivals God's claim. This was covenant law in a theocratic nation, not personal sanction.

If thou hear that certain children of Belial have withdrawn the inhabitants of their city: Let us serve other gods — thou shalt enquire, make search, ask diligently; if it be truth, smite the inhabitants, destroy it utterly. Burn the city and the spoil. It shall be an heap for ever, not built again. Nothing of the cursed thing shall cleave to thine hand. That the LORD may turn from His anger, shew thee mercy, multiply thee. Guidance: A whole city gone over to idols was treated as treason. But notice: enquire, make search, ask diligently. No vigilante mob — God demands due process before judgment. And no plunder. The goal was not gain; it was the removal of contagion.
